articles.headline: Meta to Invest $9.1 Billion in First AI Data Center in Canada, Largest Outside US
articles.summary: Meta plans to invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first AI data center in Canada, marking its largest infrastructure project outside the US. This expansion signals rising cloud costs and global competition for AI compute capacity, directly impacting enterprise AI deployment strategies and supply chain planning for international businesses.

articles.headline: Meta to Charge Developers for Tiered AI Model Access via API
articles.summary: Meta is launching a paid tier for its AI model, hosting it on its own infrastructure and selling access to developers via API. This shift introduces new cloud cost structures for businesses using open AI tools and may accelerate migration to proprietary enterprise models, affecting software pricing and operational budgets.

articles.headline: SK Hynix CEO Forecasts Worst Memory Supply Shortage in 2027 Amid AI Boom
articles.summary: SK Hynix's chief executive warns the global memory industry faces its worst supply shortage in 2027, with demand exceeding production capacity well into the next decade. This outlook threatens chip availability for AI infrastructure, raising risks for supply chain disruptions and increased memory costs for corporations and SMEs relying on high-performance computing.
